```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_balanced_parens  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

class TestIsBalancedParens:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("s", [
        "",  # Empty string
        "()",  # Simple balanced pair
        "[]",  # Different type
        "{}",  # Another type
        "(())",  # Nested same type
        "[{()}]",  # Nested mixed types
        "a(b)c",  # Balanced with non-bracket characters
        "abc",  # Only non-bracket characters
        "((a)b)",  # Balanced with nested non-brackets
        "[](){}",  # Multiple balanced pairs
    ])
    def test_balanced_cases(self, s):
        assert is_balanced_parens(s) is True

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("s", [
        "(",  # Single opening
        ")",  # Single closing
        "([",  # Unmatched opening
        ")]",  # Unmatched closing
        "({)}",  # Mismatched types
        "(()",  # Extra opening
        "())",  # Extra closing
        "(]",  # Wrong closing for opening
        "[}",  # Wrong closing for opening
        "{)",  # Wrong closing for opening
        "((a)b",  # Unbalanced with non-brackets
        "a(b)c)",  # Extra closing with non-brackets
    ])
    def test_unbalanced_cases(self, s):
        assert is_balanced_parens(s) is False

    # Edge cases are covered in the parametrized tests above, but we can add specific ones if needed
    def test_edge_empty_string(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("") is True

    def test_edge_single_opening(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("(") is False

    def test_edge_single_closing(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens(")") is False

    def test_edge_only_non_brackets(self):
        assert is_balanced_parens("hello world") is True
```
